blockingIssues.description: Tool argument schemas in src/tools/schemas.ts remain manually duplicated instead of being derived from runtime schemas via .omit/.pick/.extend, leaving drift risk and missing the feature contract.
The replacement compile-time schema-equivalence assertions are tautological because they compare schema inputs to aliases derived from those same schemas, so they cannot detect divergence.
completeRun widened its input type and casts to WorkerResult, so the transition no longer accepts a strongly typed WorkerResult boundary as required.
WorkerResultArgsSchema allows missing outcome while completeRun dereferences worker.outcome.kind on non-ok paths, creating a runtime throw path instead of a structured validation envelope.
Recorded bundle-size evidence shows dist/index.js at 993402 bytes, exceeding the required schema milestone gate threshold of <= 990000.
No bun run lint execution was recorded in the feature's gate verification evidence, leaving the regression-gate lint requirement unverified.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance that compile-time equivalence assertions must compare independently defined runtime/tool types (not aliases derived from the same schema constants), with a concrete anti-pattern/example.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-collapse-shared-schema passed handoff while tests/schema-equivalence.test-d.ts used tautological checks that could not catch schema drift.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add a schema-boundary convention that z.custom<T>() should not be used for runtime-constrained literals/enums at tool boundaries unless paired with explicit runtime refinement, because it does not validate values by default.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-collapse-shared-schema review found permissive z.custom<T>() usage in src/tools/schemas.ts that could accept invalid literals at parse boundaries.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify verification expectations for validation-only/regression-gate features: when full-suite commands are mandatory versus when scoped/targeted checks are acceptable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-single-parse-boundary and m3-schema-regression-gate both reported process mismatches between strict skill requirements and observed scoped-validation practice.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require feature verificationSteps and expectedBehavior command requirements to be reconciled with mission-level validation requirements before feature assignment (for example lint/deadcode expectations and SessionSchema.parse search scope).
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: m3-single-parse-boundary and m3-schema-regression-gate showed conflicting expectations between feature text and mission guidance, causing avoidable ambiguity during validation.

rejectedObservations.observation: core-worker red-first test authoring requirement is unsuitable for verification-only gate features.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate of the broader skill-alignment recommendation already captured under suggestedGuidanceUpdates for .factory/skills/core-worker/SKILL.md.
